mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250k signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with high initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening demand for residential construction that boosts economic growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with high housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases risk aversion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with low industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the S&P 500 forward returns signal a monetary easing cycle that boosts equity prices and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with both monetary easing and high S&P 500 returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250k and a 2-month decline in industrial production below 100 signal a weakening labor market and supply chain that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with both high initial unemployment claims and low industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million and a 2-month increase in the S&P 500 forward returns signal a strengthening demand for residential construction and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with both high housing starts and high S&P 500 returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 and a 2-month increase in the S&P 500 forward returns signal a weakening supply chain and risk aversion that reduces corporate profitability and increases equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both low industrial production and high S&P 500 returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
